commit.message:
feat: course builder, interactive lesson player, robot text chat, bug fixes

Course Builder (Admin → Курсы tab):
- Full drag-drop course editor: Course → Modules → Lessons → Blocks
- Block types: Text (markdown), Video (YouTube embed), Quiz (interactive),
  Flashcard (3D flip), Image
- AI assistant panel with subject-aware tips and quiz templates
- Publish/draft toggle, cover color picker, price, subject/level

Student Lesson Player (/courses/:id/lessons/:id):
- Sidebar with progress tracking and module navigation
- Interactive quiz with animations, score tracking, explanations
- 3D flip flashcard deck with know/don't-know flow
- Lesson completion saved to DB (CourseEnrollment, LessonCompletion)

Backend:
- Prisma: Course, CourseModule, Lesson, CourseEnrollment, LessonCompletion models
- /api/courses CRUD + enroll + progress + complete endpoints
- /api/admin/stats route (new file)
- /api/users/:id/public endpoint for public profile

Robot widget:
- Text chat input — ask questions on any page
- Voice button now works on IELTS page (was only in curator module)
- Full response shown in bubble (was truncated to 100 chars)

Bug fixes:
- Courses FiltersSidebar reset button now correctly triggers React re-render
- Courses modal "Записаться" button connected (WhatsApp / /curator / /auth)
- Public profile fetches real API data instead of mock
- Portfolio share button copies real link to clipboard
- Admin panel: dead search, bell dot, quick action buttons all fixed

Initial commit: GrandApp - Modular AI-assisted web platform

Stack:
- Backend: FastAPI + SQLAlchemy async + PostgreSQL + Alembic
- Frontend: Next.js 15 App Router + React 19 + Tailwind CSS
- AI: Anthropic Claude API integration

Features:
- Auth: JWT access/refresh token rotation, bcrypt passwords
- Users: CRUD, roles (admin/user), password change
- App Registry: CRUD + AI-powered mini-app code generation
- Bug Reports: full CRUD, severity/status management, AI clustering ready
- Feature Requests: full CRUD, voting, admin status management
- Admin dashboard: manage users, apps, bugs, features
- Scheduler-ready: AI batch analysis every 5h (APScheduler)

commit.message:
UI/UX overhaul: scoring reweight, layout polish, visual fixes

Scoring:
- Rebalance composite weights: Accessibility 35%, Street Design 25%,
  Network Design 20%, Environment 20% — reflects what walkers experience
- Move Speed Environment from Network Design → Environment component
  (vehicle speed is comfort/safety quality, not network geometry)
- Network Design sub-metrics rescaled (4 metrics, geometry-only)
- Environment sub-metrics rescaled to include Speed Environment (6 metrics)

Archetypes:
- StreetVibe: add alternative "Vibrant Urban" + "Walkable Neighborhood"
  paths using safety score, fixing "Suburban" misclassification for
  dense urban areas with weak OSM network data (e.g. Flatiron)

Display:
- SNA panel: show "—" for 0-score sub-metrics instead of red bars
- 15-Minute City score moved above MetricGrid (surfaced earlier)
- Confidence message threshold lowered to <55%, text changed to
  "Building a complete picture..."
- Local Economy bars: category-specific colours (retail, dining, etc.)
- MetricDetailPanel: warm #faf7f2 background (visually distinct)

Assets:
- Add demo.mp4 (25s edited walkthrough, ends on 15-min city 100%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix app crashes: proper error handling and auth loading guards

Root causes fixed:
- DealDetail/DealForm/DealAnalysis were firing Supabase queries before
  auth session was confirmed, causing RLS errors → redirect to '/'
- Any network/server error was treated as 'not found' and redirected
  to home, making the app feel like it crashed

Changes:
- DealDetail: wait for authLoading, show retry UI on network errors
- DealAnalysis: same pattern, catches errors without navigating away
- DealForm: wait for authLoading on edit, guard user?.id on submit,
  source field now uses datalist (works even if sources haven't loaded),
  loadSources scoped to current user's sources only
- Only PGRST116 (not found) or 42501 (permission denied) trigger redirect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix deploy issues with IAM policy and event-source mapping (#8)

* Fix Chalice prod deploy failing due to unpatched IAM policy placeholder

The deploy script was constructing policy-{stage}.json (policy-chalice-prod.json)
but the actual file configured in config.json is policy-prod.json. As a result
INJECTED_BY_DEPLOY_ATHENA_BUCKET was never replaced with the real ARN before
Chalice ran, causing a MalformedPolicyDocument error on PutRolePolicy.

Fix by reading iam_policy_file from config.json for the stage instead of
guessing the filename from the stage name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix deploy failing when chat_processor event-source mapping is Disabled

Re-enable the SQS event-source mapping automatically during post-deploy
validation instead of raising an error, then poll until it reaches
Enabled state (up to 60s).

fix(backup): harden import/export against schema version mismatches (PUNT-311) (#373)

## Summary
- Remove `.passthrough()` from Zod export schemas and add all current
model fields (as optional) to prevent unknown fields from reaching
Prisma `create()` calls
- Add `information_schema.columns` check at import start to
conditionally include fields based on what exists in the target database
- Add `select: { id: true }` to all Prisma create/update calls during
import to prevent `RETURNING *` from referencing missing columns
- Add PL/pgSQL exception handling in `wipeDatabase()` for tables (Agent,
ChatMessage, ChatSession) that may not exist
- Include Agent model in export/import pipeline with table existence
checks

## Test plan
- [ ] Import a pre-1.4.0 backup into a current schema database
- [ ] Import a current backup into a database missing newer columns
- [ ] Verify wipe handles missing tables without errors
- [ ] Verify all data imports correctly with both old and new format
backups

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add OpenAIRateLimitError to Celery autoretry_for on LLM tasks

Without this, if the key pool exhausts all retries on a 429, the
RateLimitError propagates to the Celery task but is not in autoretry_for
— so the task hard-fails without retrying and the submission stays stuck
in LLM_PROCESSING indefinitely.

Adding OpenAIRateLimitError to autoretry_for on evaluate_writing and
evaluate_speaking ensures Celery retries with backoff (up to 3×) when
all API keys are simultaneously rate-limited, giving keys time to cool
down (60s) before the next attempt. At max_retries, status → LLM_FAILED
and goes to DLQ for sweeper retry.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable issue-review and lower priority threshold in research agent

Two bugs prevented the research agent from creating new issues or reviewing
existing tickets:

1. `focus-areas` was missing `issue-review` so reviewExistingIssues() was
   never called (issues-reviewed:0 in every run log).
2. `min-priority-for-issue` was set to 'high', but industry insights are
   hardcoded to 'medium' and all detected tech debt was 'low' — nothing
   ever passed the threshold.

Fix: add issue-review to focus-areas and lower the threshold to 'medium'.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
